invariants_list.rule: The PASS command must be preceded by a USER command in the same session.
Authentication commands (USER/PASS) must be successfully completed before data transfer commands (LIST, RETR, STOR, NLST, APPE) can be issued.
A data connection setup command (PASV, EPSV, PORT, or EPRT) must precede every data transfer command (LIST, RETR, STOR).
The RNTO command must be immediately preceded by an RNFR command.
The QUIT command may be issued at any point during the session to terminate the connection.
If a PASS command returns a 530 error, the client may return to the USER state to retry authentication.
USER.username: Argument must be a username string
PASS.password: Argument must be a password string
CWD.path: Argument must be a valid directory path
XCWD.path: Argument must be a valid directory path
RETR.filename: Argument must be a valid filename or path
STOR.filename: Argument must be a valid filename or path
APPE.filename: Argument must be a valid filename or path
DELE.filename: Argument must be a valid filename or path
TYPE.type_code: Must be 'A' (ASCII) or 'I' (Image/Binary) as per RFC 959
PORT.address_port: Must follow the format h1,h2,h3,h4,p1,p2 (IP and port bytes)
MKD.dirname: Argument must be a directory name to create
XMKD.dirname: Argument must be a directory name to create
RMD.dirname: Argument must be a directory name to remove
XRMD.dirname: Argument must be a directory name to remove
RNFR.filename_from: Argument must be the existing filename to be renamed
RNTO.filename_to: Argument must be the new filename for the rename operation

recommended_actions: Several observed message types are not yet represented in the state machine; prioritize seeds and probes for: ACCT, APPE, CDUP, CWD, DELE, FEAT, HELP, MKD
Some inferred states are weakly connected in the current model; add directed seeds for: DATA_TRANSFER

suggested_campaign.objective: Increase post-auth and data-channel coverage while validating weaker transitions.

status_changes.description: INIT -> AUTH_PENDING via USER
AUTH_PENDING -> AUTHENTICATED via PASS
AUTH_PENDING -> AUTH_PENDING via PASS
AUTHENTICATED -> DATA_CHANNEL_READY via PASV
AUTHENTICATED -> DATA_CHANNEL_READY via PORT
DATA_CHANNEL_READY -> DATA_TRANSFER via LIST
AUTHENTICATED -> DATA_TRANSFER via LIST
DATA_CHANNEL_READY -> DATA_TRANSFER via RETR
AUTHENTICATED -> DATA_TRANSFER via RETR
AUTHENTICATED -> RENAME_PENDING via RNFR
RENAME_PENDING -> AUTHENTICATED via RNTO
AUTHENTICATED -> CLOSED via QUIT
INIT -> CLOSED via QUIT
AUTHENTICATED -> DATA_CHANNEL_READY via EPSV
AUTHENTICATED -> DATA_CHANNEL_READY via EPRT
AUTHENTICATED -> DATA_TRANSFER via MLSD
DATA_CHANNEL_READY -> DATA_TRANSFER via MLSD
The PASS command must be preceded by a USER command in the same session.
Authentication commands (USER/PASS) must be successfully completed before data transfer commands (LIST, RETR, STOR, NLST, APPE) can be issued.
A data connection setup command (PASV, EPSV, PORT, or EPRT) must precede every data transfer command (LIST, RETR, STOR).
The RNTO command must be immediately preceded by an RNFR command.
The QUIT command may be issued at any point during the session to terminate the connection.
If a PASS command returns a 530 error, the client may return to the USER state to retry authentication.
